seo.title: Thursday's finger-licking quick chicken - Marinda Kook

html:
There come those days when you just want to make food quickly. The days you want minimal dishes. Today is one of those days. I'm making the chicken my mom always made for us Here comes a packet of mushroom soup powder over the chicken. I know the food purists have a thing about soup powder, but you know, it's never bothered me.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 12 pieces of chicken
  • 1 onion, sliced into rings
  • 1 packet mushrooms, cut into quarters
  • 1 packet mushroom soup
  • 1 litre apricot juice
  • salt and pepper

NOW:

Pack the onion rings in an oven dish and pack the chicken on top. Season with salt and pepper.

Scatter the mushrooms over the chicken.

Sprinkle the soup powder over the mushrooms and then pour the apricot juice over everything.

 

Bake covered with foil for 30 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius. Then remove the foil and bake for another 45 minutes until nicely browned.

 

Serve the comfort food with rice, sweet pumpkin and peas. I originally made the dish for 6 people, but 4 of us ate it all up.
